title: OMOP Condition Era
notes:
In omop_condition_era, a Condition Era is defined as a span of time when the Person is assumed to have a given condition. Similar to Drug Eras, Condition Eras are chronological periods of Condition Occurrence.
Further details are provided in the Standardized Derived Elements specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#CONDITION_ERA~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Condition Occurrence
notes:
omop_condition_occurrence contains records of Events of a Person suggesting the presence of a disease or medical condition stated as a diagnosis, a sign, or a symptom.
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#CONDITION_ERA~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Death
notes:
The omop_death table contains the clinical event for how and when a Person dies.
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#DEATH~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Device Exposure
notes:
The omop_device_exposure table captures information about a person's exposure to a foreign physical object or instrument which is used for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes through a mechanism beyond chemical action.Ā
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#DEVICE_EXPOSURE~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Dose Era
notes:
A Dose Era is defined as a span of time when the Person is assumed to be exposed to a constant dose of a specific active ingredient. These are detailed in the omop_dose_era table.Ā
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Standardized Derived Elements specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#DOSE_ERA~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Drug Era
notes:
A Drug Era is defined as a span of time when the Person is assumed to be exposed to a particular active ingredient. A Drug Era is not the same as a Drug Exposure. Drug Eras are detailed in the omop_drug_era table.Ā
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Standardized Derived Elements specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#DRUG_ERA~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Drug Exposure
notes:
The omop_drug_exposure table captures records about the exposure to a Drug ingested or otherwise introduced into the body.
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#DRUG_EXPOSURE~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Measurement
notes:
The omop_measurement table contains records of Measurements, i.e. structured values (numerical or categorical) obtained through systematic and standardized examination or testing of a Person or Person's sample. The omop_measurement table contains both orders and results of such Measurements as laboratory tests, vital signs, quantitative findings from pathology reports, etc.
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#MEASUREMENT~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Note
notes:
The omop_note table captures unstructured information that was recorded by a provider about a patient in free text (in ASCII, or preferably in UTF8 format) notes on a given date. The omop_note table contains free-text causes of death from the Medical Certificate of Cause of Death (MCCD), origin and format of death record from the death registry.
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#NOTE~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Observation Period
notes:
The omop_observation_period table contains records which define spans of time during which two conditions are expected to hold: (i) Clinical Events that happened to the Person are recorded in the Event tables, and (ii) absense of records indicate such Events did not occur during this span of time.
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#OBSERVATION_PERIOD~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Observation
notes:
The omop_observation table captures clinical facts about a Person obtained in the context of examination, questioning or a procedure. Any data that cannot be represented by any other domains, such as social and lifestyle facts, medical history, family history, etc. are recorded here.
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#OBSERVATION~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Person
notes:
The omop_person table serves as the central identity management for all Persons in the database. It contains records that uniquely identify each person or patient, and some demographic information.
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#PERSON~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Procedure Occurrence
notes:
The omop_procedure_occurrence table contains records of activities or processes ordered by, or carried out by, a healthcare provider on the patient with a diagnostic or therapeutic purpose.
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#PROCEDURE_OCCURRENCE~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Specimen
notes:
The specimen domain contains the records identifying biological samples from a person. The omop_specimen table contains details of these records.
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#SPECIMEN~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Visit Detail
notes:
The omop_visit_detail table represents details of each record in the parent omop_visit_occurrence table. A good example of this would be the movement between units in a hospital during an inpatient stay.
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#VISIT_DETAIL~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.

title: OMOP Visit Occurrence
notes:
The omop_visit_occurrence table contains Events where Persons engage with the healthcare system for a duration of time.
Further details can be found in the OMOP CDM Clinical Data Tables specifications - ~Ehttps://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm53.html#VISIT_OCCURRENCE~
Due to some data quality issues identified by research groups who have been using the returned OMOP derivation dataset, the record-level tables are now restricted. Please see ~F20142~ for more information.
